raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that searches for system host name in /etc/hosts and prints the IP address in first awk field. Use hostname command to get host name. Then grep for that in /etc/hosts, then awk '{print $1}'. So: grep -w "$(hostname)" /etc/hosts | awk '{print $1}'. Might need to handle comments. Use awk to skip lines starting with #. So: awk -v host="$(hostname)" '$0 ~ host && !/^#/ {print $1; exit}'. But simpler: grep -w "$(hostname)" /etc/hosts | awk '{print $1}'. Provide one command. I'll output: grep -w "$(hostname)" /etc/hosts | awk '{print $1}'.
